channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Mozambique votes for president and the ruling party could extend its 49 years in power
item.description: MAPUTO, Mozambique (AP) — Mozambique is voting for a new president on Wednesday in an election that is expected to extend the ruling party's 49 years in power since the southern African nation gained independence from Portugal in 1975.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES MANGWIRO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: South Carolina death row inmate to choose firing squad, electric chair or lethal injection for execution
item.description: South Carolina officials told death row inmate Richard Moore that he can choose between a firing squad, the electric chair and lethal injection for his execution.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What presidential campaign? The Electoral College puts most American voters on the sidelines
item.description: WAUKEGAN, Ill. (AP) — On a table at the office of the Waukegan Township Democrats sits a box of postcards with Wisconsin addresses that were collected during a postcard-writing pizza party to help turn out voters there. Leaning against the table are homemade Harris-Walz signs.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: NASA astronaut shares timelapse video of massive Hurricane Milton from space
item.description: As Floridians brace for Hurricane Milton, a NASA astronaut shared another perspective and view of the massive storm headed for Florida from space.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How do officials plan to manage CRB infestations on Oahu?
item.description: “It’s another sad day for us, we don’t like removing trees, especially trees like the coconut," said Roxanne Adams, DPR Urban Forestry administrator.
item.dc:creator: Emily Cervantes, NewsNation

item.title: Michigan US Senate debate: Rogers, Slotkin race could determine control
item.description: Rogers and Slotkin meet on Tuesday for an hour-long debate hosted by Nexstar's WOOD-TV, beginning at 7 p.m. ET.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Van Gilder, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dolton Mayor Tiffany Henyard walks out as trustees address budget deficit
item.description: "I just want to come here and lay the law down as it relates to what's going on in our village, where we are, and where we're going," Dolton Mayor Tiffany Henyard said.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, NewsNation

item.title: October Prime Day has a ton of emergency supply deals
item.description: Everyone should have supplies on hand to ride out an emergency. And thanks to Amazon's October Prime Day, you can create a kit without breaking the bank.
item.dc:creator: Christina Marfice, NewsNation

item.title: Melania Trump waves off 'hand-swatting incident': A 'mere misunderstanding'
item.description: Melania Trump is waving off speculation that she didn't want to hold her husband's hand, chalking up images of her seemingly "swatting" away former President Donald Trump to a "mere misunderstanding.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Hurricane Milton may be October surprise in presidential battle
item.description: Strategists in both parties are looking back to Hurricane Sandy, which slammed into the mid-Atlantic states shortly before Election Day 2012.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Sally Field shares 'horrific' teenage abortion experience: 'These are the things that women are going through now'
item.description: Sally Field is opening up about her "horrific" experience of being molested while undergoing an abortion in Mexico as a teenager, saying the traumatic event is one of the reasons she's supporting Vice President Harris's White House bid.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KRON4

item.title: Bizarre laws in Washington, such as being charged with reckless driving if hugging while behind the wheel
item.description: Among Washington's strangest laws include penalties for using X-rays as shoe-fitting devices as well as trouble for intimidating another with a laser.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What polling shows about Black voters' views of Harris and Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Black registered voters have an overwhelmingly positive view of Vice President Kamala Harris, but they’re less sure that she would change the country for the better, according to a recent poll from the AP-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why is Tampa Bay so vulnerable to hurricanes?
item.description: As Milton barrels toward the Gulf Coast of Florida, experts are worried that a century of luck could come to a sudden end.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hurricane Milton is a Category 5. Florida orders evacuations and scrambles to clear Helene's debris
item.description: BELLEAIR BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Florida’s storm-battered Gulf Coast raced against a Category 5 hurricane Monday as workers sprinted to pick up debris left over from Helene two weeks ago and highways were clogged with people fleeing ahead of the storm.
item.dc:creator: HAVEN DALEY and MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: North Korea's Kim again threatens to use nuclear weapons against South Korea and US
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un warned again that he could use nuclear weapons in potential conflicts with South Korea and the United States, as he accused them of provoking North Korea and raising animosities on the Korean Peninsula, state media reported Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal court reviews civil rights lawsuit alleging environmental racism in a Louisiana parish
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A federal appellate court is reviewing a civil rights lawsuit alleging a south Louisiana parish engaged in racist land-use policies to place polluting industries in majority-Black communities. The Fifth U.S.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nation's largest water utility pauses billing amid cyberattack
item.description: The largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the United States announced Monday that it was the victim of a cyberattack, prompting the firm to pause billing to customers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Amid cyberattack, nation's largest water utility pauses billing
item.description: American Water provides services to more than 14 million people across 14 states and on 18 military installations.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: These cities are the 'safest' in America, new study says
item.description: The quality of schools, traffic and entertainment offerings are among the variety of factors you consider before moving to a new city. Another is safety.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: New analysis suggests national debt could increase under Harris, but it would surge under Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — No one is likely to be happy with the projected higher deficits laid out in a new analysis of Kamala Harris' and Donald Trump's economic plans.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Costco gold bars are selling out as prices for the precious metal break records: survey
item.description: Move over, gas and hot dogs. Costco shoppers are snatching up the store's 1-ounce gold bars despite the market's lofty price for the precious metal.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Singer Cissy Houston, mother of Whitney Houston, dies at 91
item.description: Cissy Houston, the mother of the late Whitney Houston and a two-time Grammy winner who performed alongside superstar musicians like Elvis Presley and Aretha Franklin, has died. She was 91.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Chicago mayor names new school board after entire panel resigns amid a fight over district control
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Chicago Mayor Brandon Johnson named members of a new school board on Monday, batting away criticism of the move days after all seven members resigned amid an escalating fight over control of the public schools in the nation’s third-largest city.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: You may be eligible for lawsuit payout if you ate Breyers vanilla ice cream
item.description: A court ruling on the settlement has not yet occurred, with a final approval hearing set for Nov. 21.
item.dc:creator: Alonzo Small, KRON4

item.title: Live Wobble Tracker: Map shows real-time movements of Hurricane Milton
item.description: Hurricane Milton, which reached Category 5 strength Monday, is heading right for Florida's west coast, with a potential landfall near or at Tampa Bay.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, Fox 5

item.title: President Biden approves Florida Emergency Declaration
item.description: President Joe Biden has declared a state of emergency in Florida and ordered Federal assistance to supplement State and local response efforts.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nobel Prize in medicine honors 2 scientists for their discovery of microRNA
item.description: STOCKHOLM (AP) — Two scientists won the Nobel Prize in physiology or medicine on Monday for their discovery of microRNA, tiny bits of genetic material that serve as on and off switches inside cells that help control what the cells do and when they do it.
item.dc:creator: DANIEL NIEMANN, MARIA CHENG and MIKE CORDER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Apple Watch Series 10 is slimmer and smarter
item.description: It's hard to believe the Apple Watch has been around for 10 years now. This year’s model is the Series 10 and Apple made some changes that make it thinner, lighter, and more entertaining than ever. If it’s been a while since you upgraded, this might be the year.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump intends to use military to round up opponents: Michael Cohen
item.description: “The big warning that I want people to understand is when Donald Trump says something, stop sane washing it. Stop trying to make it into something which has some normalcy to it. What he says, he intends to do.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Tennessee nurse and his dog died trying to save a man from floods driven by Helene
item.description: “I’ve never had anyone risk their life for me,” David Boutin, the man Boone McCrary had set out to rescue said. “From what I hear that was the way he always been. He’s my guardian angel, that’s for sure.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Walmart will be closed on Thanksgiving Day for 5th consecutive year
item.description: Walmart will be closed on Thanksgiving Day for the fifth year in a row, the retail giant recently announced in its holiday plans.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, Fox 5

item.title: Hurricane Milton growing stronger as it approaches Florida: NHC
item.description: Milton has strengthed into a hurricane in the Gulf of Mexico, according to the National Hurricane Center.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, ABC 4

item.title: Verdugo's go-ahead single and acrobatic catch lift Yankees over Royals 6-5 in ALDS opener
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Alex Verdugo hit a tiebreaking single in the seventh inning and saved at least one run with a sliding catch along the left-field line, boosting the New York Yankees over the Kansas City Royals 6-5 on Saturday night in their AL Division Series opener.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Elon Musk makes appearance at Trump rally casting election in dire terms
item.description: (AP) - Billionaire tech executive Elon Musk cast the upcoming presidential election in dire terms during a Saturday appearance with Donald Trump, calling the Republican presidential nominee the only candidate “to preserve democracy in America.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Child entrepreneur opens pizza shop with Caribbean twist in New York City
item.description: A young entrepreneur is bringing even more Caribbean flavor to New York City, opening a pizza shop with a Caribbean twist at 11 years old.
item.dc:creator: Zhané Caldwell, WGN 9

item.title: Helene’s powerful storm surge killed 12 near Tampa. They didn't have to die
item.description: INDIAN ROCKS BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Aiden Bowles was stubborn, so even as Florida officials told residents of the barrier island north of St. Petersburg that Hurricane Helene's storm surge could be deadly, the retired restaurant owner stayed put.
item.dc:creator: DAVID FISCHER and TERRY SPENCER, Associated Press, ABC 4
